name: Dee Lite Bakery
tagline: A Kalihi hole-in-the-wall turning out some of Honolulu's best pies, pastries, and cakes — the coconut cake is a local legend.
mustOrder: coconut cake — the signature, towering and moist
parking: Small strip mall lot on Dillingham Blvd
talkStory: The Kim family has run this unassuming Kalihi bakery for decades, building a reputation through word-of-mouth alone. The coconut cake -- towering layers of moist sponge covered in fresh shredded coconut -- is legendary for birthdays, graduations, and baby luaus. Celebrity chef Guy Fieri visited and featured their pies. Despite the fame, prices remain remarkably affordable and the strip-mall storefront has never been renovated. That's exactly how the regulars like it.
